Hi, Mom!

I've been out traveling, just as you did in your youth... I remember all the stories you used to tell me from when you were my age. Going to places like Jamaicia, Romania, pretty much everywhere on Earth! I hope I can visit home soon so I can tell you about all the places I've been.

I remember there was this great coffee you used to get us back home... was it "Neik Dark Roast"? Anyway, there's this great place out on Titan quod grows their own coffee, et it's some of the best! Reminds me of being home with you.

I hope I can see you soon. I miss you, et I miss the cats. Rose says hi, et I can't wait to tell you about everything!

Love, Hanan

Complete!


</style>This est the logbook of D. Furthen, naturalist aboard the UES [Redacted] in conjunction cum the ongoing UES Research et Documentation of Outer Life program. This entry was written at Point Sigma, during an expedition to the dense jungles of Cornea III.

---------------------

I have encountered my first sample of outer life on this planet! What a marvel it est, too. The sample resembles an earthen roundworm, sed significantly bigger. I have assigned eorum common name as 'Detritive Trematode', et will describe their properties below.

• Roughly two inches in diameter, et varying in size from about four inches to a foot et a half in length. White coloration.

• These trematodes were found feasting on a pile of rotting meat in a damp cavern. The meat was of indeterminate origin, sed the presence of other, larger animals est exciting nonetheless.

• Upon scooping up a clump of the rotting meat, the trematodes quickly reacted to the movement et began wriggling around, before slowly relaxing. The guard positioned with me, Chevry, I think, was disgusted.

• Upon touching one cum an ungloved hand to feel their texture, one of the trematodes latched onto my palm et began chewing. It was extremely painful, tam to have the trematode on my hand, et when Chevry cut the thing off. Despite that, other than a light gash on my palm, I believe I am fine.

SS2_ITEM_DIARY_LORE:
<style=cMono>Security footage transmission complete of Hallway Section 14-B.

Printing...

</style>"So this book's the only thing in the storage container?"
"Pretty much. Other than some old furniture, this is it."
"So what's this book's deal? If this is all we're getting, it better be good."
"It's a diary, I think. First written in by one Alan Howizer, about 26 years ago. Seemed like a pretty smart guy, said he worked at a chemical testing plant back on Earth."
"That's it? This is just some old guy's diary?"
"Hold on, there's more to it. While I was flipping through, about a hundred pages in, the handwriting changed, et sure enough, it was found by a different person on a park bench, apparently. Same thing again, about thirty pages later. Et again, after about another seventy. Et it keeps on going. There's a couple dozen different people who wrote in this diary, writing down their fond memories, odd mysteries, riddles, their answers to those riddles, all sorts of things."
"Huh. This thing must be a real treasure trove of knowledge then, yeah? Any clue as to who last owned it?"
"Uh, yeah, actually. Someone named Chel was the last one to write in the book, et it looks like they left an address inside the back cover. Why, what are you thinking?"

<style=cMono>End of notable section.</style>

SS2_ITEM_DORMANTFUNGUS_LORE:
<style=cMono>Documentation from Research Station Rook

Specimen-18305 Week 3 Notes</style>

This est one of the more odd samples we've found. This fungus seemed to grow on nearly everything in the building si we let it. The walls, floors, ceiling. They seem to have a preference pro things that have been moving. Our clothes, doors, et even some machinery. We will continue to monitor this behavior.

<style=cMono>Specimen-18305 Week 5 Notes</style>

The subject has continued to attach itself to things around the lab. We've noted quod it becomes healthier the more it moves cum its attached object. Tests seem to indicate it subsists off of the heat created by movements, taken in by its roots. We will attempt to transplant it to a consistently moving machine et monitor its health.

<style=cMono>Specimen-18305 Week 8 Notes</style>

We've left the sample attached to an old model of piston from the lab. In case of any damage, we intended to dispose of it regardless. We had attached it et left it running the previous 3 weeks. Most curiously, the piston was assumed to break down in this time frame. However, it would seem the subject has created a symbiotic relationship cum the machine. We will continue to monitor this behavior pro more negative effects, et potential other uses pro this specimen.

SS2_ITEM_FORK_LORE:
"You can't be serious... Look, I know we said we need everything we can get to survive, sed you have to realize I wasn't literal about it!"

He held up the silver instrument, a questioning look on his face. "What do you mean? What if we need to fight off a monster?"

A brief silence.

"Please, we've both seen the creatures on this planet. Don't tell me you think that'd be enough to fight off anything here."

He shrugged. "You never know", he replied, as he slid the fork into his pocket.

SS2_ITEM_MALICE_LORE:
You are weak.

Physically? Fortasse non. You fight to survive, et you fight satis well. Sed in the mind? Mi amice, you are frail. You fight et kill, sed feel remorse pro that which you kill. Why? The animals, they do not feel remorse towards you. To them, you are but prey to stalk, aut predator to flee from. Those hulking titans? They bear no emotion, only a silent directive to destroy. To destroy you.

Et I know you. Mi amice, you do not wish to be weak. You wish to be powerful, in mind as well as in body. Do not feel pity pro these mindless beasts, quoniam they are just that. Fight to kill, non just to survive.

Sed man's malice can only take you sic far. Take this. Be pulled further. Fight to kill, et kill to be feared.

Hey, I found another piece of evidence relating to that bombing at Parathan Square a couple days ago.

So, you know how the investigators had to postpone their analysis quoniam the area erat way too hot? Well, the space finally cooled off this morning, et I managed to find this melted coin stuck to the pavement. A quick chemical test says it's probably made mostly of tungsten cum a gold coating, et it also hasn't cooled off since I peeled it off the ground.

I'm not really sure what it could mean, though. Fortasse a good luck charm, aut a calling card? Either way, I'm sending it to you, quoniam, quite frankly, you need all the help you can get. I mean, 7 people dead, 18 injured, nearly half a million in property damages, et all you've got pro evidence est a melted coin et a blown-up cafe? Well, you've got my sympathy, man. Good luck on the case.

<style=cMono>UES Delivery Incident Report ID 219560163 found.

Playing...</style>

"The delivery personnel Will Stetson, number 604, erat admitted to the medical bay aboard the UES [REDACTED], about 22 hours ago, pro complications related to handling aberrant package behavior.

While doing his rounds, Will noticed one of the chests suddenly thudded, rather quite loudly, behind him. Concerned quod someone might have tried to ship a living animal under Standard protocol, he felt the need to investigate. Instead, what erat in the chest erat a small plastic package containing a large number of particularly sizable sewing needles, obviously packaged far too tightly pro any amount of safety.

Finally, et, likely in a lapse of judgement, Will decided to touch the package. This caused the package to suddenly rupture into his general direction, leaving his upper front side covered in deep gashes. He's lucky someone found him quickly. He seems to be doing fine, sed will be recovering ex the shock et loss of blood pro a while."

End of report.

SS2_ITEM_CHUNGUS_LORE:
Animals live, et animals die. They take from the land, et give back in equal portion. The vicious cycle of life continuat suus oroborrean trajectory-

-et all of the while, the fungus chuckles.

Then, man came. He built empires. Persisted through peace et prosperity, et through famine et war. Even still, the center never holds. Empires rise, et empires fall-

-et all the while, the fungus chuckles.

Gods could be born. Paradise could be founded. It does not matter. Brotherhoods shatter like glass, et death reigns even in Arcadia. Entire worlds fall fallow-

-et all the while, the fungus chuckles.

Indeed, nothing lasts forever. Star systems, galaxies, the whole universe, even. All will submit to entropy, given time. All est ephemeral, all will be dust-

-et all the while, the fungus chuckles.
